title: Lost tabs and history on restart (possibly due logging in to sync on another pc?)
content:
Quite simply I replaced the dead power supply on an old PC of mine and booted it up to start using it again as a work PC.

When I did the now decade old version on firefox told me I might want a clean reboot which I clicked Ok since the history was so old on it as to be irrelevant. Firefox closed but didn't reboot. So I launched it again and simply chose not to restore the old tabs when prompted (not even some of them as that was also an option). I updated the old version on firefox I had on it before restarting it and logging into my firefox account to sync and try to transfer my work tabs to the old PC. When I did so I realized on the old PC that reopening Firefox didn't reopen where I had left off. I thought it was just the update but did a check nonetheless. Opening a couple of random tabs before exiting firefox and launching it again. It didn't reopen the last tabs. I checked the settings: "open previous windows and tabs" was checked. Did a brief google search that confirmed my next step: uncheck that box > exit firefox > launch firefox > check the box again > relaunch firefox. I did all that and was still not getting my tabs back.

Then I went back to my current PC (where I had changed nothing) and missclicked (I think I was going to the settings) exiting firefox there (3 windows and probably ~60 tabs ... yes I have a problem). And now even on my main PC firefox didn't restore windows and tabs!

I hoped I could go to history > reopen closed windows but no dice. The option is greyed out. That is also when I noticed I had no history whatsoever.

Now I unfortunately relaunched firefox a couple of times here trying to troubleshoot BEFORE I went online and saw that I could have possibly restored the last session using some backups.

With all that in mind can I still restore my tabs/history somehow? And why isn't firefox picking up where I leave it even though the option for it is on?

All of this is on firefox v148.0 on win10 for both PCs if that's relevant.

Thx in advance for any and all help!

title: Is there a problem with my network?

title: Be nice and be specific. Our volunteers are Mozilla users just like you who give their own time to help. Telling us “YouTube videos are always choppy” is much more helpful than “something is broken”.
content:
1. Резюме выделенного
      1. Общее

Выделенный фрагмент содержит **цепочку из трёх X.509 TLS-сертификатов** (формат PEM), а также временну́ю метку **«5–7 минут»** (вероятно, срок действия или контекст страницы).

---

      1. Сертификат 1 — Конечный (End-Entity)

- **Субъект:** `accounts.firefox.com` - **Выдан:** Let's Encrypt (R13) - **Действителен:** 15.02.2026 — 16.05.2026 - **Тип:** TLS-сертификат сервера (Domain Validated) - **Алгоритм:** RSA 2048 / SHA-256

---

      1. Сертификат 2 — Промежуточный (Intermediate CA)

- **Субъект:** `R13` (Let's Encrypt) - **Выдан:** ISRG Root X1 - **Действителен:** 13.03.2024 — 12.03.2027 - **Назначение:** Подпись конечных сертификатов Let's Encrypt

---

      1. Сертификат 3 — Корневой (Root CA)

- **Субъект:** `ISRG Root X1` (Internet Security Research Group) - **Действителен:** 04.06.2015 — 04.06.2035 - **Назначение:** Доверенный корневой центр сертификации; самоподписанный

---

      1. Итог

Полная цепочка доверия: `accounts.firefox.com` → `Let's Encrypt R13` → `ISRG Root X1`
